title: Universal photonic artificial intelligence acceleration

data_scope.note: Exact dataset sizes and hardware specifications are in supplementary materials; benchmark datasets are pre-existing community standards

core_conclusion: A photonic AI processor achieves near-electronic numerical precision for the first time on production AI workloads — ResNet, BERT, and the DeepMind Atari deep reinforcement learning algorithm — marking photonic computing's entry into genuine competition with established electronic AI accelerators and an essential step toward post-transistor computing.
why_it_matters: Closes the longstanding precision gap that confined photonic chips to toy benchmarks; demonstrates full production-model execution (ResNet, BERT, Atari DRL) on a single chip; establishes a direct benchmark comparison against GPUs/TPUs; provides open code and datasets for reproducibility; addresses the post-Moore AI energy crisis.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary breakthrough bridging optics, semiconductor engineering, and AI; historic milestone framing (first practical-precision photonic AI chip); broad societal relevance to AI energy consumption and post-Moore computing; open reproducibility artifacts.
added_value_to_my_research: Photonic graph neural network inference: characterize precision for sparse/irregular tensor ops used in spatiotemporal GeoAI models
Low-energy on-chip fine-tuning of geospatial foundation models via photonic on-chip training extensions
Hybrid photonic-neuromorphic pipeline for energy-efficient multi-source data fusion (mobile, remote sensing, social media)
Photonic Ising machine mode for NP-hard facility-location and EV-charging optimization problems
Energy-accuracy Pareto frontier to inform model-selection for city-scale GeoAI accessibility inference
